programme.desc: The latest news in Canada and around the world.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.desc: W5 investigates romance scams and reveals a global criminal organization behind the ripoffs and tragic consequences.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Ingredients packed in cans and jars can be just as good as fresh ones.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary amps up the cravings, and savings, with recipes that prove you don't have to spend a fortune to make delicious dishes.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary will have you saying Grazie! with four deliziosa pasta recipes that keep it simple but amp up the flavour.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Pie making can be daunting, but Mary provides a pastry crash course with three different types of dough.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re working from home or at the office, Mary’s recipes mean you don’t have to sacrifice a tasty lunch for convenience.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows you how to cater to food intolerances and allergies by amping up the flavour instead of using blah ingredient substitutions.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary takes a spice-fueled adventure around the world and helps you navigate the grocery store spice aisle.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re cooking for finicky friends or tricky tots, Mary’s got it covered with strategies to feed even the pickiest eaters.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary recommends a retro theme for impressing guests, and these old-school recipes with a special Mary twist.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Allow Mary to bring your kitchen outside with delicious recipes that will demystify the grill and keep your eyebrows intact.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: A lemon raspberry loaf with a bright pink glaze, lemon Parmesan chicken.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Prepare your party platters! Mary’s handy hors d’oeuvres elevate classic recipes while keeping it simple.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary is all about cooking smarter, not harder. She shares no-sweat supermarket shortcuts to save you from kitchen time and stress.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Save your abundance of fresh herbs from wilting away with Mary’s recipes to use ‘em up in a jiff.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Celebrate the season with Mary’s recipes that take advantage of limited-edition produce, including tomatoes, zucchini, peppers and berries.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re a newbie baker or a pro who wants to take it easy, these recipes are all about supermarket hacks to raise your baking game.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the tastes of Croatia and Barbados to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong celebrates the tastes of Belgium and Madagascar to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong celebrates the tastes of Peru and Lebanon to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Chef Craig Wong combines the cuisines of South Africa and Portugal to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises South African born Shameen Miller with the results of her DNA test.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Andrea gets her DNA test results and learns about her heritage's cuisine.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Laird Hercules is surprised with the results of his DNA test.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Mary Bosco with the results of her DNA test and explores her newly revealed heritage’s cuisine.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Brazil-born Iafa Almeida Cac with her DNA test results.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: After getting the results of her DNA test, PJ Kerr explores her newfound heritage through cooking.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Bianca Osbourne with her DNA test results.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Jacob Ahee with his DNA test results.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Jesse Magallanes with his DNA test results.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Chelsea Richards discovers her newfound heritage through cooking with host Tyrone Edwards.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Carmen Araiza with her DNA results as they set off on a culinary journey.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Adopted Tara Broadbent learns about her biological heritage through food.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards surprises Denise Oilveira with the results of her DNA test.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Colombian born Martina Castillo is surprised with a culinary journey to discover her roots.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: Host Tyrone Edwards reveals the surprising results of Jamila Doncher’s DNA test with a culinary journey.

programme.title: DNA Dinners
programme.desc: After getting the results of her DNA test, Kelly Harvey-Mycula explores her newfound heritage through cooking.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shares some of his favourite recipes for a Gourmet Soup and Sammies night in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up a fresh take on a Thai-inspired meal in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows us that cooking comfort food doesn't require a full day in the kitchen.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: It'll only take you around 30 minutes to impress your loved ones with chef Spencer Watts's budget-friendly Italian feast.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Stay in with celebrity chef Spencer Watts as he prepares traditional pub dishes in under 30 minutes, for around 30 dollars.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: In under 30 minutes, chef Spencer Watts cooks up four hearty, rich, and mouth-watering French-Canadian dishes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: It's Fajita Night in host Spencer Watts kitchen as he cooks up Mexican recipes in around 30 minutes, for around 30 dollars.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Moroccan cuisine combines a million flavours and host Spencer Watts shows us how to bring that intensity home in a few simple steps.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a delicious family meal in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: It's Tandoori Take-Out night as host Spencer Watts prepares five quick and easy Indian dishes that take little time, but are big in flavour.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up a healthy and hearty meal for four that cuts down on time without sacrificing flavour.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates a Lebanese supper for four in less than thirty minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts demonstrates how to make an Indian feast for the whole family.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts prepares a delicious Chinese Take-Out meal at home in just 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up a Pizza feast that’s fast, fun, and affordable.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Thai food is on the menu as Chef Spencer Watts cooks up an affordable dinner in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes fast and affordable Japanese food for four, all in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a fresh and fragrant Vietnamese meal for four for around 30 dollars.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shares some of his favourite recipes for a Gourmet Soup and Sammies night in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Zero Gravity: Life on the International Space Station
programme.desc: ESA and NASA astronauts document six months exploring outer space at the International Space Station.

programme.title: The Age of Hubble
programme.desc: The Hubble Space Telescope has delivered unprecedented discoveries about the nature of our Universe, and more.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: Supermassive Black Hole at the Center of the Galaxy
programme.desc: The largest object in our galaxy is a supermassive black hole that dominates its central region.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: The surface of Venus is 95% carbon dioxide with an atmospheric pressure equivalent to 900 meters underwater.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: Surface temperatures reach 450 degrees - any technology we send to Venus wouldn't stand a chance in the heat.

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: Scientists are studying genomes of COVID-19 to track the disease's spread and develop lifesaving vaccines.

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: The discovery of antibiotics has saved countless lives. But how do we stay one step ahead of "super-bugs"?

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: Who can stop the menace caused by deadly mosquito-borne diseases? Mosquitoes kill a million people each year.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Oceania: The Tectonic Ring of Fire
programme.desc: Pacific and Australian tectonic plates clash across the South Pacific causing intense volcanic activity

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Ride along on dramatic balloon flights into colorful geomagnetic storms to help revolutionize space travel.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Cosmologists were stunned to learn the universe is flying apart in all directions at an ever-increasing rate.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Did Mars ever develop life? The search for answers reveals the forces that long ago doomed the Red Planet.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Examine the Universe's first micro-moments to learn why the Universe is vast almost beyond our comprehension.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: There are plans to send spacecraft to the Moon to find resources that may one day allow humans to live there.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: What are scientists learning about the history of the Moon and its relationship to the development of Earth?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: Massive solar storms attack Earth by disrupting radio signals and navigation systems and harming power grids.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim investigates a strange set of laws that link together everything from engines to humans to stars.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim learns that information isn't just about human communication; it's woven into the very fabric of reality.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Voyage of Curiosity
programme.desc: Curiosity's robot rover exploring Mars is now known as one of the greatest achievements in space exploration.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: Supermassive Black Hole at the Center of the Galaxy
programme.desc: The largest object in our galaxy is a supermassive black hole that dominates its central region.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: The surface of Venus is 95% carbon dioxide with an atmospheric pressure equivalent to 900 meters underwater.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.desc: Surface temperatures reach 450 degrees - any technology we send to Venus wouldn't stand a chance in the heat.

programme.title: Disease Hunters
programme.desc: Scientists are studying genomes of COVID-19 to track the disease's spread and develop lifesaving vaccines.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In northeastern Uganda, a herder finds that a disease affecting cattle threatens his livelihood.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: A Raika camel herd has scattered throughout the dry forest of the Rajasthan land in northwest India.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.sub-title: Living with Alpacas in the Peruvian Andes
programme.desc: The rapidly changing climate threatens the way of life for an alpaca herder in the high Andes of Peru.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: The Kalahari: a thirsty land where brief rains spark vibrant life, soon reclaimed by the desert.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa’s southern coral reefs are vibrant and dynamic worlds in which remarkable creatures that live.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa's vast grasslands host epic wildlife gatherings, where survival depends on adapting to seasons.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: See how the inhabitants of South Africa's beautiful but volatile West Coast continually adapt to survive.

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: Horses in Search of a King
programme.desc: A genetic anomaly threatens Przewalski’s wild horses. Can this rare species be saved from extinction?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: Golden Lion Tamarins narrowly escaped extinction in the 1970s, but will a new intruder spell the end?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.sub-title: The Turtle Who Came Out of Nowhere
programme.desc: Scientists are approached by a previously undiscovered species of turtle. Does this new animal pose a threat?

programme.title: Saved From Extinction
programme.desc: The oryx has been all but annihilated. Are these rare animals destined for extinction?

programme.title: The Lost Island of Madre de Dios
programme.desc: Watch divers, geologists, and biologists face extreme conditions to study this unexplored Antarctic land.

programme.title: Shark Cinema Science
programme.desc: This underseas journey explores advanced shark research worldwide with acclaimed filmmaker Andy Casagrande.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: Every year, a master shepherd takes 200 black-headed sheep through the middle of the German capital of Berlin.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In Kyrgyzstan, herders drive their herds into the mountains to pastures at nearly 2,500 meters (8,000 feet).

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On
programme.desc: In northeastern Uganda, a herder finds that a disease affecting cattle threatens his livelihood.

programme.title: Herders: The Ancient Tradition Lives On

programme.title: Saved From Extinction

programme.title: Wonders of Northern Europe
programme.desc: Finland is a country of geographic and cultural transition; at the same time with a very distinct identity.

programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 2: Fight for Survival
programme.desc: ‘White Lions – Born Wild’ is a true wildlife story, with strong characters and closely studied behaviour. We follow the cubs’ progress as they embark on the pathway to adulthood, learning to hunt, learning to avoid danger, learning to live in their white coats. This is a strong behaviour-led film, a coming-of-age story of a very rare and unusual pair of lions. Through their eyes, we learn what it is really like to be a lion in Africa.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Flying Foxes and Heat Waves
programme.desc: While the Tolga Bat Hospital is looking after endangered flying foxes, researchers are using thermographic cameras to search for flying foxes and koalas. But they can also detect bush and wildfire more quickly.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.sub-title: The Psychogramm of a Spiritual Landscape
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The Parque Nacional Peneda-Gerês is the only national park in Portugal. An area of almost 700 square kilometers, located in the extreme north of Portugal. Barren and rocky ridges in the plateaus, contrast with large forests and lush ground vegetation in deep valleys, crossed by lakes and rivers. The park is known for its oak forests, which are predominantly pedunculate and Pyrenean oaks. Among the typical animals of the park are the small Garrano ponies, a very old breed of horse and the only one in Europe that still lives in the wild.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The highest mountain range in Portugal is the Serra da Estrela. The episode embarks on a voyage of discovery to adventurous rock formations and villages steeped in tradition. The nature park is the largest protected area on the Iberian peninsula. In the mountain range we accompany the young scientist Ana Matos, who wants to breathe new life into old traditions, and cheese maker Maria Natália Lopes. She watches over more than 350 sheeps.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.sub-title: South West Portugal: Alentejano and Costa Vicentina
programme.desc: In the southwest of Portugal lies the little known Parque Natural do Sudoeste Alentejano e Costa Vicentina. It encompasses a narrow coastal strip that runs along the coast to the south of Portugal. Sparsely populated, this coastal region, which was placed under protection in 1988, is considered one of the last intact ecosystems in Europe. The steep cliffs, the almost constant wind and high waves have ensured that mass tourism has not gained a foothold here. There are still near-natural forests and a diverse bird life.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: Madeira - Portugal's autonomous island in the Atlantic Ocean, is closer to the African coast than to the mother country Portugal, from which it is separated by about 900 kilometers. The island's name comes from Portuguese sailors who claimed the uninhabited isle for themselves in 1419. Madeira means wood and that was available in abundance. Almost the entire surface was covered by dense forest. Since 1982, two-thirds of the landscape has been protected.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The "Parque Natural da Ria Formosa" is one of the largest lagoon conservation areas in Europe. Bordered by five natural islands. A mosaic of sandbanks, brackish and freshwater lagoons and salt marshes. In 2010, the "Ria Formosa" was chosen as one of the seven natural wonders of Portugal. It is a unique ecosystem: in the winter months, more than 20,000 birds gather here. In addition, the nature park has one of the largest seahorse populations internationally. More than half of all fish species in the Atlantic are said to spawn in the lagoon.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Zanzibar
programme.desc: Photographer Matthias Ziegler is an Africa specialist. He has visited nearly every country on the continent on assignments for international agencies. Zanzibar, however, is new territory for him. He's come to the former slave island for a photo book – and this time he's not alone. To see the Tanzanian archipelago from the air, he has enlisted the services of ultralight pilot Richard Meredith-Hardy of England to fly him around in his FIB, or flying inflatable boat. Perfect conditions for an adventurous journey: as a world champion ultralight pilot, Richard and his FIB can take off and land practically anywhere – in theory.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Mauritius
programme.desc: Renaud Van Der Meeren is a photographer and paramotor pilot. With his motorised parachute he goes on expeditions to the remotest corners on earth to photograph people and landscapes from the air. Renaud wanted to be a jet pilot, studied music at the Sorbonne, became a ski instructor, model and photographer's assistant before he found happiness in his current vocation. He especially likes to do his job in Mauritius – nowhere else does he find so many motifs as on the "sweetest pearl in the Indian Ocean". This time he's working on a photo book about Mauritius – by land, by water and in the air.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Madagascar
programme.desc: Spirits, Kings, Lemurs – Madagascar treats Ami to a multitude of new impressions and spectacular pictures. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied her on her trip.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Sao Tome and Principe
programme.desc: Along with photographer Rui Camilo, we travel to the West African island nation of Sao Tomé and Príncipe - in search of images that will capture the soul of this small and beautiful country.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Cape Verde
programme.desc: Cape Verde – the islands of Saudade, of longing. For Stéphane and Richard it was a journey full of adventure, unforgettable encounters and magnificent photographs. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied them on their trip.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: Wild and unknown lies the still undiscovered natural paradise of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the heart of Europe. Rough mountains, dense forests and wild waterfalls characterize the picture of the untouched landscapes. Lynx, wolves and bears roam mountains and valleys. One of the last primeval forests in Europe is a retreat for many endangered animal species. Bosnia and Herzegovina is actually an undiscovered natural paradise in the middle of Europe.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: The barren, karstic south of the country, Herzegovina, also impresses with its natural landscapes. One of them is the Livno field. Not only is it the largest wetland in the country, it is also the largest regularly flooded karst field in the world. About 700 wild horses live in this unique landscape. Their ancestors were working horses that were abandoned here more than 50 years ago. Another special feature of the karst landscape are the rivers with their tufa barriers. The countless waterfalls that pour over it are a unique natural phenomenon.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.sub-title: The Psychogramm of a Spiritual Landscape
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The Parque Nacional Peneda-Gerês is the only national park in Portugal. An area of almost 700 square kilometers, located in the extreme north of Portugal. Barren and rocky ridges in the plateaus, contrast with large forests and lush ground vegetation in deep valleys, crossed by lakes and rivers. The park is known for its oak forests, which are predominantly pedunculate and Pyrenean oaks. Among the typical animals of the park are the small Garrano ponies, a very old breed of horse and the only one in Europe that still lives in the wild.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The highest mountain range in Portugal is the Serra da Estrela. The episode embarks on a voyage of discovery to adventurous rock formations and villages steeped in tradition. The nature park is the largest protected area on the Iberian peninsula. In the mountain range we accompany the young scientist Ana Matos, who wants to breathe new life into old traditions, and cheese maker Maria Natália Lopes. She watches over more than 350 sheeps.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.sub-title: South West Portugal: Alentejano and Costa Vicentina
programme.desc: In the southwest of Portugal lies the little known Parque Natural do Sudoeste Alentejano e Costa Vicentina. It encompasses a narrow coastal strip that runs along the coast to the south of Portugal. Sparsely populated, this coastal region, which was placed under protection in 1988, is considered one of the last intact ecosystems in Europe. The steep cliffs, the almost constant wind and high waves have ensured that mass tourism has not gained a foothold here. There are still near-natural forests and a diverse bird life.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: Madeira - Portugal's autonomous island in the Atlantic Ocean, is closer to the African coast than to the mother country Portugal, from which it is separated by about 900 kilometers. The island's name comes from Portuguese sailors who claimed the uninhabited isle for themselves in 1419. Madeira means wood and that was available in abundance. Almost the entire surface was covered by dense forest. Since 1982, two-thirds of the landscape has been protected.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The "Parque Natural da Ria Formosa" is one of the largest lagoon conservation areas in Europe. Bordered by five natural islands. A mosaic of sandbanks, brackish and freshwater lagoons and salt marshes. In 2010, the "Ria Formosa" was chosen as one of the seven natural wonders of Portugal. It is a unique ecosystem: in the winter months, more than 20,000 birds gather here. In addition, the nature park has one of the largest seahorse populations internationally. More than half of all fish species in the Atlantic are said to spawn in the lagoon.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Zanzibar
programme.desc: Photographer Matthias Ziegler is an Africa specialist. He has visited nearly every country on the continent on assignments for international agencies. Zanzibar, however, is new territory for him. He's come to the former slave island for a photo book – and this time he's not alone. To see the Tanzanian archipelago from the air, he has enlisted the services of ultralight pilot Richard Meredith-Hardy of England to fly him around in his FIB, or flying inflatable boat. Perfect conditions for an adventurous journey: as a world champion ultralight pilot, Richard and his FIB can take off and land practically anywhere – in theory.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Mauritius
programme.desc: Renaud Van Der Meeren is a photographer and paramotor pilot. With his motorised parachute he goes on expeditions to the remotest corners on earth to photograph people and landscapes from the air. Renaud wanted to be a jet pilot, studied music at the Sorbonne, became a ski instructor, model and photographer's assistant before he found happiness in his current vocation. He especially likes to do his job in Mauritius – nowhere else does he find so many motifs as on the "sweetest pearl in the Indian Ocean". This time he's working on a photo book about Mauritius – by land, by water and in the air.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Madagascar
programme.desc: Spirits, Kings, Lemurs – Madagascar treats Ami to a multitude of new impressions and spectacular pictures. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied her on her trip.

programme.title: Gorilla Doctors
programme.desc: Dr. Mike Cranfield and the gorilla doctors perform check-ups, give medicine to and sometime operate on Africa's endangered mountain gorillas.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: Find out more about the sensory powers used by animals to make messages loud and clear

programme.title: Animal River Challenge
programme.desc: Rainer Bergmaz travels to the heart of Guyana's Hinterland to find the shy and rare black caiman crocodile.

programme.title: Animal River Challenge
programme.desc: Rainer Bergomaz searches for the Eagle in the canopy of Guyana's unspoiled rainforest.

programme.title: Germany's Underworld
programme.sub-title: From the Alps to the River Rhine
programme.desc: In this episode, we meet powerful hunters like the pike and sinister meat-eating plants like the bladderwort.

programme.title: Germany's Underworld
programme.sub-title: From the Harz Mountain to the North Sea
programme.desc: In this episode meet the ancient Atlantic sturgeon, the ground beetle, the sea eagle, and a family of beavers.

programme.title: Endless Horizon
programme.desc: Namibia offers a chance to see Africa's wildlife, including elephants, lions, and hippos, in its savannahs.

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: Megeti
programme.desc: A lone Ethiopian Wolf battles the elements and other predators as she tries to unite with a pack.

programme.title: Bear Whisperer
programme.desc: Follow Bear Whisperer Steve Searles in Mammoth Lakes, California, where bears and tourists collide.

programme.title: The Real Lost World
programme.desc: Follow a team of adventurers on an epic journey to the plateau of Roraima, Venezuela, to The Real Lost World.

programme.title: Addos Elephants
programme.desc: Protected elephants live and thrive in Addo Elephant National Park.

programme.title: Equator Specials
programme.desc: The Equatorial sun burns deep into the lives of animals across three continents

programme.title: Equator Specials
programme.desc: In three vastly different equatorial zones life is a finely balanced between extremes of heat and cold.

programme.title: Koala Rescue
programme.desc: Koala Rescue showcases the resilience and determination of Australians who bravely rescued

programme.title: Lioness in Exile
programme.desc: Manyari, the Queen, leaves her pride with her cubs to escape two young threats who want to kill them.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson, animal lover, faces the challenge of relocating 28 lions and 14 hyenas.

programme.title: Baboon Bandits
programme.desc: Meet Jimmy, Fred and Merlin, the alpha males of the Smits Baboon Gang in Cape Town, South Africa.

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 01
programme.desc: The Galapagos Archipelago's diverse biodiversity is attributed to the Pacific's Humboldt

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 02
programme.desc: home to diverse creatures, inspired Charles Darwin's evolution theory due to their unique adaptations

programme.title: Chandani - The Daughter Of The Elephant Whisperer
programme.desc: Chandani, a girl in a male profession, takes on the role of a Mahout, supported by ranger Mohammed

programme.title: Saliega's Lineage, The Return Of The Iberian Lynx
programme.desc: The Iberian lynx was saved by captive females San Diego and Ora, and their newborns.
